items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Staigue Fort
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Kerry
*Type:* fortified house
*Period:* Iron Age/Early Medieval
*Coords:* 51.80527778, -10.01583333
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staigue_stone_fort
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Staigue%20Fort/@51.775,-10.028,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/staigue-fort.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Staigue or Staig is a ruined stone ringfort eighteen kilometres west of Sneem, on the Iveragh peninsula in County Kerry, Ireland. The fort is thought to have been built during the late Iron Age, probably 100 BC, as a defensive stronghold for a local lord or king.

_Listing 1 of 111_

listing.name: Staigue Fort
listing.desc: Staigue or Staig is a ruined stone ringfort eighteen kilometres west of Sneem, on the Iveragh peninsula in County Kerry, Ireland. The fort is thought to have been built during the late Iron Age, probably 100 BC, as a defensive stronghold for a local lord or king.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Segedunum Roman Fort
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Tyne and Wear
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Roman (2nd century)
*Coords:* 54.98791, -1.53231
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Segedunum
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Segedunum%20Roman%20Fort/@54.99,-1.5317,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/segedunum-roman-fort.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.northeastmuseums.org.uk/segedunum

*Description:*
Segedunum was a Roman fort at modern-day Wallsend, North Tyneside in North East England. The fort lay at the eastern end of Hadrian's Wall near the banks of the River Tyne. It was in use for approximately 300 years from around 122 AD to almost 400. Today Segedunum is the most thoroughly excavated fort along Hadrian's Wall, and is operated as Segedunum Roman Fort, Baths and Museum. It forms part of the Hadrian's Wall UNESCO World Heritage Site.

_Listing 2 of 111_

listing.name: Segedunum Roman Fort
listing.desc: Segedunum was a Roman fort at modern-day Wallsend, North Tyneside in North East England. The fort lay at the eastern end of Hadrian's Wall near the banks of the River Tyne. It was in use for approximately 300 years from around 122 AD to almost 400. Today Segedunum is the most thoroughly excavated fort along Hadrian's Wall, and is operated as Segedunum Roman Fort, Baths and Museum. It forms part of the Hadrian's Wall UNESCO World Heritage Site.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Lewes Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* south east london
*County:* East Sussex
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 11th century
*Coords:* 50.8729, 0.0076
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lewes_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Lewes%20Castle/@50.8743,0.0072,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZM8tYSbLhVMUujcftjUUEye2CrGUARX8-QJXsxffkRyk8BTZhQHye1ZQz9OiWdTydGsSXqcPVLMtGbyR85sI9w_LUD7pbTVIwRXHI9DBndtb_L2jdJUTvmiISo3T3CPaY8NEEcr1oQsztCoUx4=s4800-w1080
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Lewes Castle is a medieval castle in the town of Lewes in East Sussex, England. Originally called Bray Castle, it occupies a commanding position guarding the gap in the South Downs cut by the River Ouse and occupied by the towns of Lewes and Cliffe. It stands on a man-made mount just to the north of the high street in Lewes, and is constructed from local limestone and flint blocks.

_Listing 3 of 111_

listing.name: Lewes Castle
listing.desc: Lewes Castle is a medieval castle in the town of Lewes in East Sussex, England. Originally called Bray Castle, it occupies a commanding position guarding the gap in the South Downs cut by the River Ouse and occupied by the towns of Lewes and Cliffe. It stands on a man-made mount just to the north of the high street in Lewes, and is constructed from local limestone and flint blocks.

imageAttribution.description: Two towers of Lewes Castle, West Sussex, photographed from the west.

*Description:*
Michelham Priory is the site of a former Augustinian Priory in Upper Dicker, East Sussex, England, United Kingdom. The surviving buildings are owned and administered by the Sussex Archaeological Society and are Grade I and Grade II listed.
